title: Election 2024 live updates: Harris picks Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz as her running mate, AP sources say
summary:
Five years before Hillary Clinton chose Tim Kaine as her running mate, an individual purchased the domain ClintonKaine.com. He attempted to sell it to the campaign but was unsuccessful. The domain was later acquired by the Trump campaign and used to promote anti-Clinton news.

The same individual now owns the domain WalzHarris.com. He has expressed support for Minnesota Governor Tim Walz as Harris' potential running mate. The website currently features a blank chartreuse background with Walz's name in lowercase black letters.

Despite his prior experience with the Clinton campaign, the individual has no reservations about selling the WalzHarris.com domain to the Harris campaign. He believes that the Harris campaign has sufficient funds to acquire the domain and that selling it is a business decision comparable to renting out a billboard.

title: Slow-moving Tropical Storm Debby could bring "catastrophic flooding" to parts of Georgia and the Carolinas, forecasters say
summary:
Tropical Storm Debby, now weakened from a hurricane, crawls towards Georgia and the Carolinas. It brings intense rainfall, prompting concerns of catastrophic flooding in the region. The storm has already claimed five lives in Florida and Georgia, leading to emergency declarations in all three states impacted.

Forecasters predict historic rainfall totals of up to 25 inches, which could cause widespread flooding in Georgia, South Carolina, and North Carolina. The storm is expected to move offshore of Georgia later Tuesday and drift offshore until Thursday before moving inland over South Carolina.

Authorities in South Carolina are calling Debby an "unprecedented event" and have issued flood warnings and curfews in Savannah and Charleston. The storm has already spawned tornadoes in Monck's Corner and Edisto Beach, causing damage to buildings and vehicles. Flash flooding has also occurred in Florida, prompting the rescue of hundreds of people from submerged homes.
